verses.text: The people assemble fasting, in sackcloth, separated from foreigners, and spend a fourth of the day reading the law and a fourth confessing and worshiping. Repentance is given real time and place. A people in earnest set apart hours to hear God's word and own their sin.

The people assemble fasting, in sackcloth, separated from foreigners, and spend a fourth of the day reading the law and a fourth confessing and worshiping. Repentance is given real time and place. A people in earnest set apart hours to hear God's word and own their sin.

The people assemble fasting, in sackcloth, separated from foreigners, and spend a fourth of the day reading the law and a fourth confessing and worshiping. Repentance is given real time and place. A people in earnest set apart hours to hear God's word and own their sin.

The Levites cry aloud to God and call the people, 'Stand up and bless the LORD your God for ever and ever.' Confession opens into praise. Even on a day of mourning for sin, the heart turns to bless the God whose mercy makes repentance possible.

The Levites cry aloud to God and call the people, 'Stand up and bless the LORD your God for ever and ever.' Confession opens into praise. Even on a day of mourning for sin, the heart turns to bless the God whose mercy makes repentance possible.

They praise the LORD as the only God, maker of heaven, earth, and all their host, who chose Abram and gave the covenant of the land. The prayer begins with who God is. To remember His greatness and faithfulness rightly frames every plea that follows.

They praise the LORD as the only God, maker of heaven, earth, and all their host, who chose Abram and gave the covenant of the land. The prayer begins with who God is. To remember His greatness and faithfulness rightly frames every plea that follows.

They praise the LORD as the only God, maker of heaven, earth, and all their host, who chose Abram and gave the covenant of the land. The prayer begins with who God is. To remember His greatness and faithfulness rightly frames every plea that follows.

They recall the deliverance at the Red Sea, where God divided the waters and cast the pursuers into the deep. His past redemption is rehearsed. Remembering how God saved before strengthens faith that He will save again.

They recall the deliverance at the Red Sea, where God divided the waters and cast the pursuers into the deep. His past redemption is rehearsed. Remembering how God saved before strengthens faith that He will save again.

They recall the deliverance at the Red Sea, where God divided the waters and cast the pursuers into the deep. His past redemption is rehearsed. Remembering how God saved before strengthens faith that He will save again.

They recount the pillar of cloud and fire, the law given at Sinai, the manna, and water from the rock. God led, taught, and fed them at every step. The history of His care is a treasury that feeds the gratitude of His people.

They recount the pillar of cloud and fire, the law given at Sinai, the manna, and water from the rock. God led, taught, and fed them at every step. The history of His care is a treasury that feeds the gratitude of His people.

They recount the pillar of cloud and fire, the law given at Sinai, the manna, and water from the rock. God led, taught, and fed them at every step. The history of His care is a treasury that feeds the gratitude of His people.

They recount the pillar of cloud and fire, the law given at Sinai, the manna, and water from the rock. God led, taught, and fed them at every step. The history of His care is a treasury that feeds the gratitude of His people.

Yet the fathers grew proud, would not obey, and made a molten calf, 'but thou... forsookest them not.' Their sin is owned, but His patience shines brighter. The record of rebellion only magnifies the mercy that would not let them go.

Yet the fathers grew proud, would not obey, and made a molten calf, 'but thou... forsookest them not.' Their sin is owned, but His patience shines brighter. The record of rebellion only magnifies the mercy that would not let them go.

Yet the fathers grew proud, would not obey, and made a molten calf, 'but thou... forsookest them not.' Their sin is owned, but His patience shines brighter. The record of rebellion only magnifies the mercy that would not let them go.

For forty years God led them in the wilderness, His pillar never failing, so that they lacked nothing and their clothes did not wear out. His provision was constant and complete. The God who guides His people through the desert supplies their every need.

For forty years God led them in the wilderness, His pillar never failing, so that they lacked nothing and their clothes did not wear out. His provision was constant and complete. The God who guides His people through the desert supplies their every need.

For forty years God led them in the wilderness, His pillar never failing, so that they lacked nothing and their clothes did not wear out. His provision was constant and complete. The God who guides His people through the desert supplies their every need.

He gave them kingdoms, multiplied their children, and brought them into a rich land full of good things they had not earned. Grace heaped blessing upon blessing. All they possessed was a gift, that they might serve the Giver.

He gave them kingdoms, multiplied their children, and brought them into a rich land full of good things they had not earned. Grace heaped blessing upon blessing. All they possessed was a gift, that they might serve the Giver.

He gave them kingdoms, multiplied their children, and brought them into a rich land full of good things they had not earned. Grace heaped blessing upon blessing. All they possessed was a gift, that they might serve the Giver.

He gave them kingdoms, multiplied their children, and brought them into a rich land full of good things they had not earned. Grace heaped blessing upon blessing. All they possessed was a gift, that they might serve the Giver.

Yet they rebelled, killed the prophets, and were given to their enemies; when they cried, He delivered, only for them to sin again. The sad cycle repeats. Man's heart returns to folly, but God's mercy returns to save again and again.

Yet they rebelled, killed the prophets, and were given to their enemies; when they cried, He delivered, only for them to sin again. The sad cycle repeats. Man's heart returns to folly, but God's mercy returns to save again and again.

Yet they rebelled, killed the prophets, and were given to their enemies; when they cried, He delivered, only for them to sin again. The sad cycle repeats. Man's heart returns to folly, but God's mercy returns to save again and again.

God testified against them by His Spirit in the prophets, yet for His great mercy did not utterly consume them or forsake them. Judgment was tempered by compassion. Even in chastening, God remembers that they are His and spares a remnant.

God testified against them by His Spirit in the prophets, yet for His great mercy did not utterly consume them or forsake them. Judgment was tempered by compassion. Even in chastening, God remembers that they are His and spares a remnant.

God testified against them by His Spirit in the prophets, yet for His great mercy did not utterly consume them or forsake them. Judgment was tempered by compassion. Even in chastening, God remembers that they are His and spares a remnant.

'Now therefore, our God... let not all the trouble seem little,' they pray, confessing God just and themselves wicked, who did not serve Him in their good land. They justify God and condemn themselves. True confession lays all the blame where it belongs — on the sinner, never on God.

'Now therefore, our God... let not all the trouble seem little,' they pray, confessing God just and themselves wicked, who did not serve Him in their good land. They justify God and condemn themselves. True confession lays all the blame where it belongs — on the sinner, never on God.

'Now therefore, our God... let not all the trouble seem little,' they pray, confessing God just and themselves wicked, who did not serve Him in their good land. They justify God and condemn themselves. True confession lays all the blame where it belongs — on the sinner, never on God.

'Behold, we are servants this day' in the land God gave their fathers, its increase going to foreign kings for their sin; and so they make and seal a sure covenant. Confession ends in fresh commitment. Repentance that is real binds itself by solemn vow to obey.
